identity.short_name: Degree-4 irreducibles over F3 with parity-projection irreducible over F2

subdomain(s): constrained coefficient patterns via digitwise reduction

problem_spec.summary: Count degree-4 polynomials P over F_3 that are irreducible over F_3, subject to the constraint that the polynomial Q over F_2 formed by reducing each coefficient of P (viewed as an integer in {0,1,2}) modulo 2 via pi(1)=1 and pi(0)=pi(2)=0 has degree 4 and is irreducible over F_2. Output the count as a three-digit zero-padded integer.
givens: pi: {0,1,2} -> F_2 given by pi(0)=0, pi(1)=1, pi(2)=0 (coefficientwise, using the unique reps 0,1,2).
Q(x) = sum_{i=0}^4 pi(a_i) x^i in F_2[x], where P(x)=sum_{i=0}^4 a_i x^i with a_i in F_3.
Constraints: deg(Q)=4 and Q is irreducible over F_2.
Constraint: P is irreducible over F_3.
ask: Determine N = #{ P in F_3[x] : deg(P)=4, P irreducible over F_3, deg(Q)=4, Q irreducible over F_2 }.
Output N as a three-digit, zero-padded integer.

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: degree where reducibility splits are only 1+3 or 2+2

parameter(s).name: projection_rule
parameter(s).purpose: digitwise coefficient constraint linking P to Q

invariant(s).name: leading_and_constant_for_degree4_Q
invariant(s).statement: If deg(Q)=4 and Q is irreducible over F_2, then Q is monic with constant term 1; hence pi(a_4)=1 and pi(a_0)=1, forcing a_4=1 and a_0=1 in F_3 (since only 1 maps to 1 under pi).

invariant(s).name: forced_coefficients_from_projection
invariant(s).statement: For i=1..3: if Q has coefficient 1 at x^i then a_i=1 in F_3; if Q has coefficient 0 at x^i then a_i is restricted to {0,2} in F_3.

invariant(s).name: irreducible_degree4_over_field_iff_no_linear_or_irreducible_quadratic_factor
invariant(s).statement: Over any field, a degree-4 polynomial is reducible iff it has a linear factor or an irreducible quadratic factor (since the only nontrivial degree splits are 1+3 and 2+2; a product of two reducible quadratics would already have a linear factor).

essential_assumption(s): The projection is the set-map on representatives {0,1,2} followed by mod 2, not a ring homomorphism F_3 -> F_2.
Irreducibility is tested in F_3[x] and F_2[x] respectively.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The invariants force a_4=a_0=1 and restrict (a_1,a_2,a_3) to a finite discrete set. Listing all irreducible Q of degree 4 over F_2 and lifting them produces a complete finite candidate set. A complete factor-sieve (roots in F_3 plus all monic irreducible quadratics over F_3) decisively accepts or rejects each candidate, yielding a unique count N.

technique(s).name: count_irreducibles_over_F2_via_gauss_formula
technique(s).purpose: establish the number of monic irreducible quartics over F_2 and justify that a short explicit list is exhaustive
